key_topics: Counting and cardinality: numbers to 100
Number words: one through twenty
Place value: ones and tens
Comparing quantities: more/less/equal
Addition facts: sums to 20
Subtraction facts: differences from 20
Doubles and near-doubles
Story problems (word problems) with addition and subtraction
Ordinal numbers: 1st through 10th
Telling time: to the hour and half-hour
Counting coins: pennies, nickels, dimes, quarters
Measuring with non-standard and standard units (inches)
Geometry: squares, rectangles, triangles, circles
Introduction to fractions: one-half
Graphs: reading simple bar graphs and pictographs
competences: Count to 100 by 1s, 2s, 5s, and 10s
Know addition and subtraction facts to 20
Tell time to the hour and half-hour
Identify basic 2D shapes and sort by properties
Solve simple word problems with addition and subtraction
typical_mistakes: Reverting to counting all instead of counting on
Confusing dimes and nickels by value

key_topics: Phonics and Decoding: Core Knowledge Language Arts (CKLA) phonics sequence
Short vowels: CVC words (cat, dog, sit, hot, cut)
Consonant blends and digraphs
Long vowels: CVCe pattern (cake, bike, cone)
High-frequency words (Dolch and Fry word lists)
Reading connected text with fluency
Comprehension: retelling stories with beginning, middle, end
Writing: composing sentences and short paragraphs
Capitalisation and punctuation: periods, question marks
Handwriting: print letter formation
Listening and speaking: telling and listening to stories
Grammar: nouns (common and proper), verbs in sentences
competences: Decode CVC and CVCe words fluently
Read grade-appropriate text with accuracy and expression
Retell stories identifying beginning, middle, and end
Write complete sentences with capital letters and end punctuation
Identify common and proper nouns
typical_mistakes: Confusing short vowel sounds (especially short e and short i)
Not applying the silent e rule consistently

key_topics: World Geography: continents and oceans
Maps: compass rose, key, symbols
American History: Columbus and Early Explorers
Native Americans: major tribes and cultures
Early American settlements: Jamestown, Plymouth
Thanksgiving: Pilgrims and Wampanoag
American Symbols: Statue of Liberty, Liberty Bell, bald eagle
Presidents: George Washington, Abraham Lincoln
World History: Ancient Egypt — pharaohs, pyramids, mummies
Mesopotamia: Tigris and Euphrates, early civilization
competences: Name and locate continents and oceans on a map
Use a map key and compass rose
Identify key American historical figures and their contributions
Describe basic features of Ancient Egypt

key_topics: Animals and Their Needs: food, water, shelter, air
Classification: mammals, birds, reptiles, fish, amphibians
Insects: structure (head, thorax, abdomen), life cycle
Plants and Their Needs: sunlight, water, nutrients
Plant life cycle: seed, germination, seedling, plant, flower, fruit
Human Body: major organs — heart, lungs, stomach, brain
Senses: five senses and sense organs
Weather: measuring temperature, wind, rain
Seasons: characteristics of each season
Sky: Sun, Moon, stars — day and night cycle
competences: Classify common animals by class (mammal, bird, etc.)
Describe the plant life cycle from seed to seed
Identify major human body organs and their functions
Describe weather and explain the four seasons
typical_mistakes: Thinking plants get food from soil alone

key_topics: Music: singing simple songs, keeping a beat, dynamics (loud/soft)
Music: recognising common instruments: violin, trumpet, piano, flute
Visual Arts: elements of art — line, shape, colour, texture
Art techniques: drawing, painting, collage
Art appreciation: looking at famous works (Van Gogh, Picasso basics)
Folk songs: American folk traditions
competences: Keep a steady beat and recognize dynamics
Identify common musical instruments by sight and sound
Use basic art elements in own artwork
